summary: Comprehensive test suite covering all task requirements: version parsing (multiple formats, error cases), conventional commit parsing (all types, breaking change detection via ! and footer, scope, multiline bodies), semantic version bumping (major/minor/patch/none), changelog generation with proper grouping and file prepending, integration via CLI orchestration, and end-to-end workflow validation. Tests use actual file I/O, temporary isolated directories, and execute the full GitHub Actions pipeline via act with 3 critical test cases. Edge case coverage is solid (invalid inputs, empty inputs, file state preservation, suppressed types) but missing some error scenarios (malformed JSON, permission errors, nonexistent directories). Test organization is clean with modules separated by concern, clear naming conventions, proper isolation via afterEach cleanup, and an excellent approach to fixture management in act.test.ts. Minor style issue (inline require() in test). Would reliably catch regressions in the versioning pipeline; confidence is high due to exact value assertions and real-world workflow execution testing.
